content.problemStatement:
In this task, you will create a static Morse code translator. Morse code is a method of encoding text characters as standardized sequences of two different signal durations, called dots and dashes. Your task is to implement a static method that can translate a given string of alphanumeric characters to Morse code.

The Morse code dictionary for alphanumeric characters is provided in the code. You need to implement a static method called `to_morse` that takes a string as input and returns the Morse code representation of that string.

For example, if the input is "HELLO 123", the output should be ".... . .-.. .-.. --- / .---- ..--- ...--"

Note:


The input string will contain only uppercase letters and numbers.
Words in the input string are separated by a single space.
In the Morse code output, use a forward slash (/) to separate words.
Use a single space to separate Morse code characters within a word.
constraints: The string contains only uppercase English letters (A-Z), digits (0-9), and spaces.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a string of alphanumeric characters.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print the Morse code representation of the input string.

sampleTestCases.explanation: First line translates to "HELLO WORLD" and second line to "SOS 2021" using the standard Morse mapping, with spaces between symbols and " / " between words.
G (--.), E (.), E (.), K (-.-) form the first word; digits 1,2,3 become .----, ..---, ...--.
Single word "CODE" is translated character by character without any slash.

solution.approach: The problem can be solved by creating a dictionary that maps each alphanumeric character to its Morse code equivalent. Then, for each test case, iterate over the input string, and for each character, append its Morse code equivalent to the result string. If the character is a space, append a forward slash (/) to the result string to separate words.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T * N), where T is the number of test cases and N is the maximum length of the input string.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(N), where N is the maximum length of the input string.
